content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agents can be redesigned to tie their curiosity-driven exploration signal directly to the utility of memory compression and summarization, where an agent only actively queries for new environmental data when its compressed long-term memory fails to accurately predict unexpected outcomes, rather than querying based on raw prediction uncertainty alone. This creates a self-reinforcing loop where improving long-term memory retention shapes what new knowledge the agent chooses to acquire.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
